Acceptance criteria (AC-2, AC-4):

- A Zod schema exists that validates dashboard card configs at runtime.

Test requirements:

- If any gap is found, document it and coordinate with SPEC-154 owner before proceeding.
- This task BLOCKS T-017, T-022, T-023..T-028 — do not start those until this passes.

tasks.title: ADMIN billing blast-radius audit
tasks.description:
Enumerate every admin billing route and every admin billing page in the codebase. For each one, verify it checks `BILLING_METRICS_VIEW` or `SUBSCRIPTION_VIEW_ALL` permission (via `PermissionEnum`) rather than hard-coding the ADMIN role. Produce a written finding documenting each route/page and its current permission check.

Files to audit:
- `apps/api/src/routes/admin/billing/` (all route files)
- `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/billing/` and related pages
- Any middleware or guards that gate the billing section
- `packages/seed/src/required/rolePermissions.seed.ts` (current ADMIN billing perms)

Acceptance criteria (§3 Permission changes, AC-28, AC-29):
- Written finding exists documenting each billing route/page with its permission check.
- Finding explicitly states whether ADMIN role is hard-checked anywhere (flag = blocker).
- Finding confirms or denies that permission check is via `PermissionEnum` throughout.

Test requirements:
- This is an audit task — the output is a written finding document, not code.
- The finding must be committed before T-015 can start.
- BLOCKS T-015 (seed change to revoke billing perms from ADMIN). Do NOT commit T-015 until this audit passes.

tasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/protected/host/favorites/breakdown route
tasks.description:
Build a new protected API route returning per-accommodation bookmark counts scoped to the authenticated host.

Files to create/modify:
- `apps/api/src/routes/protected/host/favorites-breakdown.ts` (new route handler)
- Register in `apps/api/src/routes/protected/host/index.ts`
- Uses existing `countBookmarksForEntity()` from `@repo/db` or service layer

Route spec (AC-17):
- `GET /api/v1/protected/host/favorites/breakdown`
- Auth: requires authenticated session with HOST role
- Scope: `ownerId` automatically injected from `ctx.user.id` — NOT from query params
- Response shape: `{ data: [{ accommodationId: string, slug: string, bookmarkCount: number }] }`
- Empty array if host has no accommodations

Test requirements (AC-17):
- Integration test file: `apps/api/src/routes/protected/host/__tests__/favorites-breakdown.test.ts`
- Test: authenticated HOST user returns only their own accommodations
- Test: response shape matches documented interface exactly
- Test: unauthenticated request returns 401
- Test: non-HOST role returns 403

tasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/protected/host/conversations/response-rate route
tasks.description:
Build a new protected API route computing conversation response rate and average response time, scoped to the authenticated host.

Files to create/modify:
- `apps/api/src/routes/protected/host/conversations-response-rate.ts` (new route handler)
- Register in `apps/api/src/routes/protected/host/index.ts`
- Aggregates over `conversations` table fields: `ownerMessageCount`, `firstGuestMessageAt`, `firstOwnerReplyAt` where `ownerId = ctx.user.id`

Route spec (AC-18):
- `GET /api/v1/protected/host/conversations/response-rate`
- Auth: requires authenticated session with HOST role
- Scope: `ownerId` injected from auth context
- Response shape: `{ data: { responseRatePct: number, avgResponseTimeMinutes: number } }`
- `responseRatePct`: (conversations with owner reply / total conversations) * 100, rounded to 1 decimal
- `avgResponseTimeMinutes`: mean of (`firstOwnerReplyAt` - `firstGuestMessageAt`) for replied conversations
- Returns `{ responseRatePct: 0, avgResponseTimeMinutes: 0 }` when no conversations

Test requirements (AC-18):
- Integration test file: `apps/api/src/routes/protected/host/__tests__/conversations-response-rate.test.ts`
- Test: correct pct and avg with seeded conversation data
- Test: returns zeros when no conversations exist
- Test: scope isolation — HOST A cannot see HOST B's rates
- Test: 401/403 for unauthenticated/wrong role

tasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/admin/newsletter/subscribers/by-preference route

Route spec (AC-19):

- Auth: requires `NEWSLETTER_SUBSCRIBER_VIEW` permission
- Response shape: `{ data: { OFFERS: number, EVENTS: number, GUIDES: number, PRODUCT_NEWS: number } }`
- Counts subscribers whose preferences include each category (a subscriber can appear in multiple categories)
- Returns zeros for categories with no subscribers

Test requirements (AC-19):

- Test: correct counts per preference category with seeded subscribers
- Test: only active subscribers are counted

- Test: response shape matches documented interface

tasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/admin/posts/trend route
tasks.description:
Build a new admin API route returning the post publication trend for the last 12 months.

Files to create/modify:
- `apps/api/src/routes/admin/posts/trend.ts` (new route handler)
- Register in `apps/api/src/routes/admin/posts/index.ts`
- Date-grouped aggregation over `posts.createdAt` for the last 12 months

Route spec (AC-20):
- `GET /api/v1/admin/posts/trend`
- Auth: requires `POST_VIEW` or equivalent admin permission
- Response shape: `{ data: [{ month: string, count: number }] }` where `month` is `'YYYY-MM'` format
- Array has exactly 12 entries covering the last 12 calendar months (including current)
- Months with zero posts return `{ month: 'YYYY-MM', count: 0 }`
- Ordered chronologically (oldest first)

Test requirements (AC-20):
- Integration test: `apps/api/src/routes/admin/posts/__tests__/trend.test.ts`
- Test: returns 12 entries
- Test: correct counts for months with seeded posts
- Test: zero-fill for months without posts
- Test: 403 without required permission

Route spec (AC-22):

tasks.title: Integration tests for all 9 aggregation routes
tasks.description:
Write comprehensive integration tests for each of the 9 new 🟡 aggregation routes (T-003 through T-011). One test file per route, each asserting shape, authentication, and scope isolation.

This task is a consolidation/hardening pass — each individual route task (T-003..T-011) includes its own inline test requirements. T-012 ensures all 9 are green as a group and adds cross-cutting edge cases.

Files to create (if not already created by T-003..T-011):
- One `__tests__/*.test.ts` per route in its respective directory

Acceptance criteria (AC-17 through AC-25):
- All 9 routes pass their shape assertions
- All 9 routes pass their auth assertions (401 unauthenticated, 403 wrong permission)
- HOST routes assert scope isolation (no cross-owner data leaks)
- Trend routes assert 12-entry arrays with correct `YYYY-MM` format
- All tests run in under 30s total

Test requirements:
- Use the project's standard integration test harness (Vitest + db test setup)
- AAA pattern throughout
- No `.only()` or hard-coded `.skip()` in committed code

tasks.title: Create dashboard source resolver skeleton
tasks.description:
Scaffold the `dashboard-sources.ts` registry with: role context provider hook, typed resolver interface, and query key factory. No actual source registrations yet — just the infrastructure.

Files to create:
- `apps/admin/src/lib/dashboard-sources.ts` — main registry
- `apps/admin/src/contexts/DashboardContext.tsx` (or similar) — role/user context provider

Key types to define (AC-13, AC-16):
```typescript
type SourceId = string;
type DashboardContext = { userId: string; role: string; permissions: string[] };
type ResolvedSource = { queryKey: readonly unknown[]; queryFn: () => Promise<unknown> };
type SourceResolver = (ctx: DashboardContext) => ResolvedSource;
```

Query key convention (AC-16):
- All dashboard query keys must include role and scope: `['dashboard', role, sourceId, scopeId?]`
- 60s `staleTime` set as default in resolver output

Test requirements:
- Unit test asserting the registry returns a resolver for a given sourceId
- Unit test asserting query key includes role identifier

tasks.title: Create DeferredWidget component
tasks.description:
Build a reusable `DeferredWidget` component that renders a 'coming soon' placeholder for any 🔴 data slot, without erroring and without blocking other slots in the same card.

Files to create:
- `apps/admin/src/components/dashboards/widgets/DeferredWidget.tsx`

Component interface:
```typescript
interface DeferredWidgetProps {
  phaseSpec: string;        // e.g. 'SPEC-159'
  description: string;      // Human-readable description of what will appear here
  expectedLabel?: string;   // Optional: label of the future data point
}
```

Acceptance criteria (AC-11):
- Renders a visually consistent 'coming soon' state (use same card padding, same skeleton-like appearance or a clear 'Próximamente' badge)
- Accepts `phaseSpec` and `description` props
- Does NOT throw errors
- Does NOT block rendering of other slots in the same card
- Uses i18n-safe labels (`es`/`en`/`pt`)

Test requirements (AC-11):
- Unit test: renders without errors given valid props
- Unit test: displays phaseSpec reference in rendered output
- Unit test: renders independently of any data fetching (no query calls)

tasks.title: Write editorDashboard config
tasks.description:
Define the `editorDashboard` configuration object in `apps/admin/src/config/ia/dashboards.ts` for the EDITOR role: 8 cards (A–H) with source IDs and tri-locale labels.

Files to create/modify:
- `apps/admin/src/config/ia/dashboards.ts`

Cards to define (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3):
- Card A: Posts (KPI + list)
- Card B: Eventos (KPI + list)
- Card C: Suscriptores Newsletter (KPI 🟢, KPI+breakdown 🟡, DeferredWidget 🔴→SPEC-160)
- Card D: Campañas Newsletter (list)
- Card E: Estadísticas blog (KPI + chart 🟡, DeferredWidget 🔴→SPEC-159)
- Card F: Estadísticas eventos (KPI, DeferredWidget 🔴→SPEC-159)
- Card G: Salud (checklist — computed client-side from editor.posts.list + editor.events.list)
- Card H: Comentarios (list)

Requirements (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3):
- Exactly 8 card configs
- Each card passes SPEC-154 Zod schema
- Deferred slots reference correct SPEC numbers

Test requirements (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3):
- Tested by T-033 CI validation suite
- Card count assertion: `editorDashboard.cards.length === 8`

tasks.title: Write adminBaseDashboard config
tasks.description:
Define the `adminBaseDashboard` configuration object in `apps/admin/src/config/ia/dashboards.ts` for the ADMIN role: 7 cards (A–G) with source IDs and tri-locale labels.

Files to create/modify:
- `apps/admin/src/config/ia/dashboards.ts`

Cards to define (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3, AC-5):
- Card A: Estadísticas de entidades (6 KPI widgets)
- Card B: Alojamientos (list)
- Card C: Editorial (list — events + posts)
- Card D: Crons (list 🟢, DeferredWidget 🔴→SPEC-161 for failed/last-run)
- Card E: Estado del sistema (status widget 🟢, maintenance flag 🟡)
- Card F: Pendiente de moderación (KPI + breakdown 🟡)
- Card G: Usuarios (KPI+breakdown 🟡 + chart 🟡)

Requirements (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3, AC-5):
- Exactly 7 card configs
- ADMIN (not SUPER_ADMIN) resolves to this config
- Passes SPEC-154 Zod schema

Test requirements (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3, AC-5):
- Tested by T-033 CI validation suite
- Card count assertion: `adminBaseDashboard.cards.length === 7`

tasks.title: Write superAdminOnlySection config
tasks.description:
Define the `superAdminOnlySection` configuration object in `apps/admin/src/config/ia/dashboards.ts` for SUPER_ADMIN-only cards: 2 cards (H–I) with `onMissing: 'hide'` permission gates.

Files to create/modify:
- `apps/admin/src/config/ia/dashboards.ts`

Cards to define (AC-1, AC-2, AC-4, AC-5):
- Card H: Audit Logs — ALL 3 slots deferred (DeferredWidget→SPEC-162 for audit log and security log; DeferredWidget→SPEC-163 for Sentry errors); gated with `AUDIT_LOG_VIEW` permission + `onMissing: 'hide'`
- Card I: Estadísticas de billing — billing KPIs + chart from SUPER billing sources; gated with `BILLING_METRICS_VIEW` permission + `onMissing: 'hide'`

Requirements (AC-4, AC-5, AC-31, AC-32):
- Exactly 2 card configs
- Both gated with `onMissing: 'hide'` so ADMIN users do not see them
- ADMIN role → 0 visible cards from this section
- SUPER_ADMIN role → 2 visible cards

Test requirements (AC-4, AC-5, AC-31, AC-32):
- Tested by T-033 CI validation suite and T-039 gating tests
- Card count assertion: `superAdminOnlySection.cards.length === 2`
- Both cards have `onMissing: 'hide'` in config

tasks.title: CI config validation test suite
tasks.description:
Write a Vitest suite that validates all four dashboard config objects against the SPEC-154 Zod schema and asserts card counts match the spec exactly.

Files to create:
- `apps/admin/src/config/ia/__tests__/dashboards.test.ts`

Acceptance criteria (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3):
- `hostDashboard` passes Zod schema and has exactly 7 cards
- `editorDashboard` passes Zod schema and has exactly 8 cards
- `adminBaseDashboard` passes Zod schema and has exactly 7 cards
- `superAdminOnlySection` passes Zod schema and has exactly 2 cards
- All source IDs in all configs are registered in `dashboard-sources.ts`
- All cards have unique IDs within their config
- All cards have `label.es`, `label.en`, `label.pt` (non-empty strings)
- Both SUPER-only cards have `onMissing: 'hide'` (AC-4)

Test requirements:
- Runs in CI on every PR that touches `apps/admin/src/config/ia/`
- No mocks — tests the real config objects against the real Zod schema
- Fail message must identify WHICH card failed validation (include card id in assertion message)

tasks.title: Build dashboard renderer component
tasks.description:
Build the main dashboard renderer component that reads the current user's resolved config(s), fires all source queries in parallel, dispatches to the appropriate widget type renderers, and includes the 'Actualizar' button.

Files to create:
- `apps/admin/src/components/dashboards/DashboardRenderer.tsx`

Behavior (AC-5, AC-33, AC-34, AC-35):
- Role resolution: ADMIN → `adminBaseDashboard`; SUPER_ADMIN → `adminBaseDashboard` + `superAdminOnlySection`; HOST → `hostDashboard`; EDITOR → `editorDashboard`
- Fires all source queries in parallel using TanStack Query's `useQueries` (no waterfall — AC-34)
- Each card renders in its own Suspense boundary so one slow card doesn't block others
- 'Actualizar' button calls `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['dashboard', role] })` (AC-33)
- Cards sharing the same TanStack Query prefix key re-use cached data (AC-35)
- Widget type dispatch: `kpi` → KpiWidget, `list` → ListWidget, `chart` → ChartWidget, `checklist` → ChecklistWidget, `status` → StatusWidget, deferred slot → DeferredWidget
- `onMissing: 'hide'` gating evaluated against current user's permissions

Test requirements (AC-33, AC-34, AC-35):
- Unit test: ADMIN resolves to 7 cards
- Unit test: SUPER_ADMIN resolves to 9 cards
- Unit test: 'Actualizar' button triggers invalidation
- Unit test: cards without user permission are hidden when `onMissing: 'hide'`

tasks.title: Migrate dashboard page to per-role renderer
tasks.description:
Replace the content of `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/dashboard.tsx` to use `DashboardRenderer`. Keep the old `useDashboardStats()` path active (commented out or behind a feature flag) until parity is confirmed by T-036.

Files to modify:
- `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/dashboard.tsx` — replace implementation, keep old hook available

Acceptance criteria (AC-36):
- Route renders `DashboardRenderer` as its primary content
- Old `useDashboardStats()` hook remains importable (not deleted yet)
- Page header, breadcrumbs, and layout structure are preserved
- Route guards and auth checks are unchanged

Test requirements (AC-36):
- Manual verification: navigate to `/dashboard` as ADMIN, HOST, EDITOR, and SUPER_ADMIN — confirm correct cards appear per role
- No TypeScript errors on the modified page file
- Existing route-level test (if any) still passes

tasks.title: Parity verification for 6 ADMIN KPIs
tasks.description:
Write and pass an integration test asserting that the 6 ADMIN KPI values (accommodations, destinations, events, posts, attractions, users) returned by the new dashboard renderer match those from the old `useDashboardStats()` on the same seeded database.

Files to create:
- `apps/admin/src/__tests__/dashboard-parity.test.ts` or in the integration test suite

Acceptance criteria (AC-37):
- All 6 KPI values match between old and new data sources:
  - accommodations count
  - destinations count
  - events count
  - posts count
  - attractions count
  - users count
- Test runs against a seeded database (not mocks) to catch any aggregation discrepancy
- Test passes with both code paths active (old hook and new renderer)

Test requirements (AC-37):
- Integration test using the standard db test harness
- AAA: Arrange = seed DB; Act = call both old and new data paths; Assert = values match
- Must produce a clear failure message identifying WHICH KPI diverged and by how much
- BLOCKS T-037 (do not delete `useDashboardStats()` until this test is green)

tasks.title: Delete useDashboardStats hook
tasks.description:
After T-036 parity test passes, delete the old `useDashboardStats()` hook and confirm no other consumers reference it.

Files to delete/modify:
- `apps/admin/src/features/dashboard/useDashboardStats.ts` (delete)
- Any import of `useDashboardStats` in other files (remove or replace)

Acceptance criteria (AC-38):
- `useDashboardStats` file is deleted
- No TypeScript compile errors after deletion
- No other file in the codebase imports `useDashboardStats`
- All tests still pass after deletion

Test requirements (AC-38):
- Run full typecheck after deletion: `pnpm typecheck`
- Run tests after deletion: `pnpm test`
- Grep codebase for `useDashboardStats` — zero results expected

tasks.title: HOST scope isolation tests
tasks.description:
Write tests asserting that all HOST-scoped dashboard queries inject `ownerId` and that no global count leaks to HOST users.

Files to create:
- `apps/admin/src/__tests__/dashboard-host-scope.test.ts`

Acceptance criteria (AC-14):
- For each HOST source ID registered in T-018, assert that `ownerId` is included in the query key
- For each HOST source ID, assert that the query function URL includes the current user's ID as scope parameter
- Simulate two HOST users with different accommodations; assert each user's query returns only their own data

Test requirements (AC-14):
- Unit test per source: query key includes `[..., userId]`
- Integration test: HOST user A's response for `host.accommodations.list` does not include HOST user B's accommodations
- Integration test: HOST user A's `host.favorites.breakdown` returns only their listings' counts
- Scope injection must come from auth context, not from query params (verify by checking the queryFn, not just the URL)

tasks.title: SUPER gating tests
tasks.description:
Write tests asserting that ADMIN users see 7 cards and SUPER_ADMIN users see 9 cards, and that toggling permissions renders or hides the super-only section correctly.

Files to create:
- `apps/admin/src/__tests__/dashboard-super-gating.test.ts`

Acceptance criteria (AC-31, AC-32, AC-5):
- ADMIN role → `adminBaseDashboard` → 7 visible cards
- SUPER_ADMIN role → `adminBaseDashboard` + `superAdminOnlySection` → 9 visible cards
- ADMIN user: cards H and I are NOT rendered (not in DOM)
- SUPER_ADMIN user: cards H and I ARE rendered
- Simulate stripping `BILLING_METRICS_VIEW` from SUPER_ADMIN → card I hides (`onMissing: 'hide'`)
- Simulate stripping `AUDIT_LOG_VIEW` from SUPER_ADMIN → card H hides

Test requirements (AC-31, AC-32):
- Unit test: renderDashboard with ADMIN role → 7 card elements in DOM
- Unit test: renderDashboard with SUPER_ADMIN role → 9 card elements in DOM
- Unit test: card H not present when ADMIN role
- Unit test: card I not present when ADMIN role
- Unit test: card I hidden when BILLING_METRICS_VIEW removed from user permissions

tasks.title: Performance baseline tests
tasks.description:
Write tests asserting that all dashboard queries fire in parallel (no waterfall) and that initial render completes in under 500ms on a warm cache.

Files to create:
- `apps/admin/src/__tests__/dashboard-performance.test.ts`

Acceptance criteria (AC-33, AC-34, AC-35):
- All queries for the current role's dashboard are initiated within the same render cycle (no waterfall)
- Initial render completes in < 500ms with warm cache
- Multiple cards querying the same underlying entity share a TanStack Query key prefix and do not make duplicate network requests

Test requirements (AC-33, AC-34, AC-35):
- Unit test: use `useQueries` spy to assert all queries initiated in a single render
- Unit test: measure time from mount to last query initiation — assert < 500ms
- Unit test: ADMIN cards A (entities count) and B (accommodations list) share `['dashboard', 'admin', 'accommodations']` prefix — assert no duplicate fetches
- Use vitest fake timers for the 500ms assertion

tasks.title: Deferred-placeholder rendering tests
tasks.description:
Write tests asserting that each 🔴 slot renders `DeferredWidget` without errors and that the remaining 🟢/🟡 slots in the same card are unaffected.

Files to create:
- `apps/admin/src/__tests__/dashboard-deferred.test.ts`

Acceptance criteria (AC-11):
- Every deferred (🔴) slot renders `DeferredWidget` component
- The `DeferredWidget` receives the correct `phaseSpec` prop for each slot:
  - HOST card G views slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-159'`
  - EDITOR card C open-rate slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-160'`
  - EDITOR card E views slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-159'`
  - EDITOR card F views slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-159'`
  - ADMIN card D failed/last-run slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-161'`
  - SUPER card H audit log slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-162'`
  - SUPER card H security log slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-162'`
  - SUPER card H Sentry slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-163'`
- In each mixed card, the non-deferred slots render their data correctly alongside DeferredWidget
- No errors thrown when DeferredWidget is rendered

Test requirements (AC-11):
- Unit test per deferred slot: assert DeferredWidget rendered with correct phaseSpec
- Unit test: HOST card G live KPI slots render alongside deferred views slot
- Unit test: DeferredWidget renders in isolation without any query calls
